message:
fix(db_fec): remove manual K..K' zero-padding injection in attempt_page_repair (closes #83)

`attempt_page_repair` previously walked from `k_usize..params.k_prime`
and injected synthetic source symbols for the RFC 6330 padding domain:
for each padded source index it built a `ReceivedSymbol` with the
constraint-matrix row's columns and coefficients, an `is_source =
true` flag, and a zero-filled data block of `page_size` bytes. This
mirrored what older asupersync versions required from callers.

asupersync 0.3.x changed contract: `InactivationDecoder::decode`
synthesizes the K..K' padding rows internally (see
`implicit_padding_rows` in `asupersync-0.3.1/src/raptorq/decoder.rs`
around line 1212; consumed in the decode accumulation around line
1688). Source ESIs are now validated against the real K domain, so
caller-supplied padding symbols with `esi >= K` are rejected and
`decode` errors out before producing any output.

The fix is to delete the manual padding-row construction. The
`params`, `base_rows`, and `constraints` locals that only existed
to build those rows are removed alongside it. The `received` vector
is now populated solely from the constraint symbols and the
caller-provided erased / received source symbols, exactly as
asupersync 0.3.x expects.

A short comment now points at the lines in asupersync's decoder that
own the padding-row work, so future readers don't reintroduce the
manual injection thinking `decoder.decode` "needs" K' rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
